questions.question: What military step did the U.S. take after JD Vance failed to secure a peace deal with Iran in Islamabad?
options: It began a naval blockade of ships entering or leaving Iranian ports
It launched ground troops into Tehran
It withdrew all U.S. forces from the Persian Gulf
It transferred control of the Strait of Hormuz to the U.N.
questions.explanation: The U.S. began a naval blockade of ships entering or leaving Iranian ports after the talks failed. Trump said the move was meant to stop what he called Iranian “extortion.”

questions.question: What did the divided federal appeals court do in the dispute over deportation flights carrying Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador?
options: It ordered the Trump administration to return all deported migrants immediately
It upheld Judge Boasberg's contempt investigation in full
It shut down Judge Boasberg's contempt investigation
It transferred the case directly to the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The 2-1 D.C. Circuit ruling ended Boasberg’s contempt probe, calling it an abuse of discretion and saying his earlier order was not clear enough to support criminal contempt.

questions.question: Which senator was the only Republican to support the Democratic resolution to force an end to U.S. military action in Iran without congressional approval?
options: John Thune
Thom Tillis
Rand Paul
Marco Rubio
questions.explanation: Rand Paul was the lone Republican to back the resolution, while John Fetterman was the only Democrat to oppose it. Senate Republicans blocked the measure for the fourth time.

questions.question: About how many Haitians in the U.S. would have Temporary Protected Status restored for three years under the bipartisan House bill?
options: About 35,000
About 120,000
About 350,000
About 1 million
questions.explanation: The bill would restore Temporary Protected Status for about 350,000 Haitians, along with work permits and deportation protections. It passed the House with support from 11 Republicans.

questions.question: What did Pete Hegseth recite during a Pentagon prayer service?
options: The Lord's Prayer from the Gospel of Matthew
A made-up Bible verse from Pulp Fiction
A prayer written by George Washington
Psalm 23 in full
questions.explanation: Hegseth read a fake prayer he called “CSAR 25:17,” which echoed the fictional verse from Pulp Fiction rather than an actual biblical passage. The Pentagon later defended him and said it was “obviously inspired” by the movie.
